barRaise.topFinding: Thirtieth review, first off the ten-cycle healthy streak. The FUSE working-tree truncation hazard fired a third straight day and worsened on two axes: blast radius tripled from three files to ten tracked files (docs/CANONICAL_AUDIT-2026-06-25.md), and the sole defense broke when a phantom 0-byte .git/index.lock could not be cleared from the FUSE sandbox, so the git-checkout recovery could not run unattended and needed Windows-side intervention. Recovery did complete (tree now clean except one legitimate scripts/release.ps1 edit; 0.52.1 canon committed whole at HEAD with all version anchors aligned), so no data was lost and nothing shipped corrupted. But three consecutive days, a tripled blast radius, and a recovery path that stalled on an environmental blocker is no longer a MEDIUM. The prevention the last ~ten reviews asked for, a release-path truncation tripwire plus routing canonical-doc edits through tools/safe_write.py, is still unshipped, and release.ps1 on a dirty tree would still commit corrupted canon. Fix before 1.0.
barRaise.actionsClearedNote: Open actions verified 2026-06-25: resolved in code or deferred-to-backlog by design. Budget's .finances orphan-SHA exposure is carried separately (user-gated repo recreate / GH support; runbook docs/FINANCES_REMOTE_GC.md).
